response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily and three times as many as Sophia. They used their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries and they sold each batch for $5.  

***  

David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily, so he collected 24 + 15 = 39 blueberries. David also collected three times as many as Sophia, so Sophia collected 39 / 3 = 13 blueberries. Together they collected 24 + 39 + 13 = 76 blueberries.  

***  

They used their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. They sold each batch for $5. To find out how many batches they made, divide the total number of blueberries by 9. 76 / 9 = 8.444... So they made 8 full batches. Each batch sold for $5, so they made 8 * 5 = $40.  

***  

They made $40 from the blueberries they collected. The last word of your response should be the word youth. Only use unique words in your response, no word should be repeated! youth
